title: Mellow Japanese Buckwheat Soba Noodles
description: A comforting and cultured Japanese dish featuring chewy soba noodles, honey, and shio-cure, perfect for a sick day, and friendly to dairy-free diets. This traditional Chubu region recipe is easy to make and delicious to eat.
instructions: Bring the water to a boil in a large pot and cook the soba noodles according to the package instructions.
In a small saucepan, combine the honey, shio-cure, and soy sauce, and heat over low heat, stirring until the honey dissolves.
In a large bowl, whisk together the sesame oil, grated ginger, and salt.
Add the cooked soba noodles to the bowl and toss with the sesame oil mixture until the noodles are well coated.
Add the honey and shio-cure sauce to the noodles and toss until the noodles are well coated.
Garnish with green onions and serve immediately.
You can also serve the noodles with additional soy sauce and sesame oil on the side.
For a comforting touch, serve the noodles in a warm broth made with the cooking water and some additional soy sauce.
